prompt: a cozy candlelit bedroom with an open storybook, moonlight streaming through the window, a peaceful and magical atmosphere, warm tones, fairytale illustration style
text: Welcome to Dream and Listen, where magical stories help you drift peacefully to sleep. Tonight, we journey into a timeless tale of curses, fairies, and love — The Sleeping Beauty. Imagine this: if you woke up after 100 years of slumber, what would the world look like? Let’s find out together…

prompt: a joyful royal couple in regal clothing holding a swaddled baby princess in a grand palace chamber, sunbeams pouring through stained-glass windows, soft focus, serene and warm ambiance
text: Long ago, a kind king and queen longed for a child. After many years, their prayers were answered — a baby girl was born, radiant as the sun. The whole kingdom celebrated the miracle. They named her Aurora, and planned a great feast in her honor.

prompt: a royal banquet hall filled with six fairies in glowing, elegant gowns, each blessing the baby princess with shimmering magic, a cradle at the center surrounded by sparkles, warm and magical atmosphere
text: To the celebration came six good fairies, each bringing a magical gift: beauty, grace, joy, wisdom, music, and kindness. As the final blessing was about to be given, a cold wind blew through the hall…

prompt: a dark, mysterious fairy with a dramatic cloak arriving uninvited, casting a long shadow across the bright hall, startled guests and glowing candles, dramatic lighting
text: An uninvited guest entered — the seventh fairy, forgotten and feared. With bitterness in her voice, she cursed the child: On her fifteenth birthday, Aurora would prick her finger on a spindle… and die. Gasps echoed. But the sixth fairy, though unable to undo the curse, changed it: Aurora would not die — but fall into a deep sleep for a hundred years, until awakened by true love’s kiss.

prompt: a royal town crier announcing a new law, servants collecting spinning wheels, piles of wheels being burned or locked away, sunset light, medieval town square
text: The king, desperate to save his daughter, ordered every spinning wheel in the land destroyed. Time passed. Aurora grew into a beautiful and gentle young woman, beloved by all — unaware of the curse that lingered in her fate.

prompt: Princess Aurora in a quiet tower room, looking curiously at a spinning wheel glowing with soft light, surrounded by dust and cobwebs, enchanted dreamy lighting
text: On the eve of her fifteenth birthday, while exploring the castle, Aurora discovered an old spiral staircase. At the top, in a dusty room, she found an old woman spinning. Curious, Aurora reached out — and pricked her finger. She collapsed instantly, and the curse took hold.

prompt: entire castle falling asleep instantly: cooks frozen mid-task, animals still, royal guards dozing at their posts, soft glowing light and creeping vines starting to grow
text: The moment she fell, so did the whole castle. Maids dozed where they stood, the king and queen slumped upon their thrones. The fairies returned, casting protective magic over the realm as the forest grew thick and wild, hiding the castle for a hundred years.

prompt: a young prince in armor riding through a dense enchanted forest, thorns and vines parting magically, dappled sunlight and mysterious glow, hopeful fairytale mood
text: Generations passed. One day, a brave young prince heard the legend of a sleeping beauty hidden beyond a cursed forest. With sword in hand and hope in his heart, he journeyed through the brambles, guided by fate — until at last, he reached the silent castle.

prompt: the prince gently leaning toward a peacefully sleeping princess in a sunlit tower room, soft magical glow as she awakens, innocent and enchanting fairytale style
text: In the highest tower, he found her — just as the stories said. He knelt, took her hand, and kissed her softly. Her eyes fluttered open. The curse was broken. The castle awakened, and sunlight poured into every hall as joy returned to the kingdom.

prompt: a joyful wedding in a blooming royal garden, the prince and princess walking among smiling guests and fairies, birds flying, soft golden light, storybook illustration style
text: The prince and princess were married in a garden overflowing with joy. The six good fairies returned to bless them once more. And so, Aurora’s story reminds us: even after the longest sleep, love and hope always awaken what was lost.

prompt: a glowing castle under a starry sky, the prince and princess looking out from a tower balcony, enchanted forest glowing faintly below, peaceful dreamy mood
text: What if you woke up 100 years in the future — what would you dream of seeing first? Tell us in the comments. And don’t miss the full audiobook version of Sleeping Beauty on our channel — you won't believe how it ends...
